equipment.subtitle: Account-scoped equipment templates used by recipes (snapshot copy; no live references).

errors.mashEfficiencyRange: Mash efficiency must be between 0 and 100.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.subtitle: Adjust contrast, text size, and spacing. Saved on this device; if you are signed in, settings also sync to your account.
accessibility.note: Tip: your browser zoom also works, and respects locale ({locale}).

acidRequired.title: Acid required (target pH)
acidRequired.body:
Conceptual model:
- We solve for an acid amount that brings Starting pH -> Target pH, given Starting alkalinity and water volume.

Notes:
- If the selected profile has no pH, we only use the manually entered Starting pH.
- Acid type/strength changes the conversion from equivalents to mL/grams.
- This is a heuristic calculator: use it as guidance, then measure/adjust.

finalAlkalinity.title: Final alkalinity (ppm as CaCO3)
finalAlkalinity.body:
Meaning:
- Alkalinity after the calculated acid addition.

Notes:
- It can be near 0 when acid neutralizes the buffering capacity.
- We report alkalinity in ppm as CaCO3 (standard brewing convention).

ionsAfterSalts.title: Resulting ions (after salts only)
ionsAfterSalts.body:
Meaning:
- Starting from the selected sparge profile, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams at the given sparge water volume.

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.

ionsAfterSaltsAndAcid.title: Resulting ions (after salts + acid)
ionsAfterSaltsAndAcid.body:
Meaning:
- Same as “after salts”, then apply the acid result.

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

alkalinityHeuristic.title: Heuristic: Ca/Mg reduce effective alkalinity
alkalinityHeuristic.body:
We apply a residual-alkalinity-style adjustment:
- Calcium and magnesium from salts modestly reduce effective alkalinity, so acid required can change slightly when salts are added.

Notes:
- This is intentionally modest; it improves realism without overfitting.

acidRequired.title: Acid required (target pH)
acidRequired.body:
Conceptual model:
- We solve for an acid amount that brings Starting pH -> Target pH, given Starting alkalinity and mash water volume.

Notes:
- Acid type/strength changes the conversion from equivalents to mL/grams.
- This is a heuristic calculator: use it as guidance, then measure/adjust.

finalAlkalinity.title: Final alkalinity (ppm as CaCO3)
finalAlkalinity.body:
Meaning:
- Alkalinity after the calculated acid addition.

Notes:
- It can be near 0 when acid neutralizes the buffering capacity.
- We report alkalinity in ppm as CaCO3 (standard brewing convention).

ionsAfterSalts.title: Resulting ions (after salts only)
ionsAfterSalts.body:
Meaning:
- Starting from the mixed source water, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams.

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.
- This table does not include acid; see the overall result for combined output.

overallSnapshot.title: Overall mash snapshot (salts + acid)
overallSnapshot.body:
Meaning:
- Combined result after applying both salts and acid.

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

ionsAfterSalts.title: Resulting ions (after salts only)
ionsAfterSalts.body:
Meaning:
- Starting from the mixed source water, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams.

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.

overallSnapshot.title: Overall boil snapshot (salts + acid)
overallSnapshot.body:
Meaning:
- Combined result after applying both salts and acid.

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

mergedWaterRecap.title: Merged water recap
mergedWaterRecap.body:
Meaning:
- This section summarizes each stream (mash/sparge/additional boil) and the merged summary.

Notes:
- Merged pH is an approximate volume-weighted [H+] mix (rule-of-thumb).
- Streams without saved snapshots are excluded from merged ions.

mergedIons.title: Merged ions (ppm)
mergedIons.body:
Meaning:
- Computed from saved mash/sparge/boil snapshots after salts + acid (SO4/Cl counter-ions only) and averaged by volume.

Notes:
- Streams without saved snapshots are excluded.
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

recipes.title: Recipes
recipes.subtitle: This is the recipe area entrypoint (list/edit/water calculator).

import.title: Import recipe
import.subtitle: Import a recipe file and create a new recipe in your account.
import.singleSubtitle: Import one recipe file into your account (you choose the style).
import.bulkSubtitle: Import a file containing multiple recipes and create them all at once.
import.formatNotResolved: Unable to detect format from filename. Choose a format above.
import.bulkStyleRule: Bulk import matches style to BJCP 2021 by exact name first, then code. If no match, we assign Custom.

errors.unknownFormat: Unknown format. Please choose BeerJSON or BeerXML.
errors.previewMissing: Preview response is missing expected data.
errors.importMissingId: Import response is missing recipe id.

edit.title: Edit recipe
edit.shapeNote: Single-page editor with section navigation. Water chemistry is a link-out to the dedicated calculator page.
edit.notReadyToLoad: Not ready to load this recipe.

equipmentSection.help: Choose an account-level equipment profile to snapshot into this recipe (no live reference).

edit.hopsHelp: Pick hops from the database (or enter manually). Stored as a snapshot on the recipe.
edit.yeastHelp: Select yeast from the database (or enter manually). Stored as a snapshot on the recipe.
edit.waterHelp: The full mash chemistry + water calculator lives on its own page (not embedded here).

analysis.help: Derived estimates. If required inputs are missing, values show as “Insufficient data”.

mash.title: Mash water
mash.adjustmentHint: Select a source + dilution profile and set volumes to see mixed ions.
mash.gristSummaryHelp: We avoid duplicating the full grist table here. Use the recipe editor for details; this page only keeps a snapshot for calculations.
mash.overallResultHeading: Overall mash water result (HCO3 derived from alkalinity)

sparge.title: Sparge water
sparge.saltAdditionsHelp: Base profile is the selected sparge water profile above. Add salts in grams; we compute resulting ions (ppm) for the sparge water volume.

boil.title: Additional boil water
boil.overallResultHeading: Overall boil water result (HCO3 derived from alkalinity)
boil.adjustmentHelp: Choose source/target/dilution profiles and volumes to compute a mixed starting water profile.
boil.saltAdditionsHelp: Select a Source profile and set Source volume. Dilution is optional, but if Dilution volume is > 0 you must select a Dilution profile.
boil.saltAdditionsBaseHelp: Base profile is the mixed source water above. Add salts in grams; we compute resulting ions (ppm).

delete.confirmBody: This cannot be undone. Click “Delete now” to confirm, or Cancel to keep it.

export.title: Export
export.subtitle: Download strict BeerJSON for one recipe or all recipes.
export.strictNote: Exports are strict BeerJSON (internal addition row ids are removed for interoperability).

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Desktop-first web app + native mobile apps, offline-first brew-day logging.

importExport.title: Import / Export
importExport.supportedNote: Recipe import/export interoperability is centered on BeerJSON (canonical).
importExport.customImportCta: Contact us for affordable custom import.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: For custom import requests, send us a message and we’ll reply with next steps and an estimate.

waterProfiles.title: Water profiles
waterProfiles.createdProfilesStartUnverified: Created profiles start as unverified. Use the table actions to verify/unverify.

waterHub.title: Water management
waterHub.missingHeaders: Missing dev headers. Go to the dashboard and click Save headers (User + Active account), then come back here.
waterHub.recapSubtitle: Uses saved snapshots where available; merged pH is an approximation using volume-weighted [H+].
waterHub.mergedIonsDescription: Merged ions (ppm) are computed from saved mash/sparge/boil snapshots after salts + acid (SO4/Cl counter-ions only) and averaged by volume. Streams without saved snapshots are excluded.
waterHub.noMergedProfile: No merged profile available yet (need saved salts + acid snapshots for at least one stream).
waterHub.finalRecapSubtitle: A quick, heuristic summary (not a full water chemistry model).
waterHub.finalRecapCaveat: Caveat: RA is a heuristic; results depend on assumptions and do not replace a full mash pH/chemistry model.
waterHub.styleExpectedRaPale: Pale / hop-forward styles usually benefit from lower RA.
waterHub.styleExpectedRaAmber: Amber / malty styles often tolerate moderate RA.
waterHub.styleExpectedRaDark: Dark / roasty styles often benefit from higher RA.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Brewery App is an internal tool for recipe development and brew-day logging, with a water chemistry calculator inspired by BrunWater, Troester and others. Beer.json schema fully supported. Raw material database of choice for v0 is BeerProto.
about.translationsRowPrefix: Translations are welcome and managed via our contribution workflow (see
about.translationsSideNote: Side note: this is just a primer — the translation contribution workflow is planned, but not fully in place yet.

selectAccount.title: Select account
selectAccount.subtitle: Choose which brewery/account you want to work in.

auth.noteBody: English is the original language. Other languages (starting with Italian) will soon be 100% supported.

i18nContributing.title: Help translate (i18n contributing)
i18nContributing.subtitle: We welcome translation contributions. English is the source of truth; Italian is the first community language.
i18nContributing.howItWorks1: The web app uses locale-prefixed URLs (e.g. /en/..., /it/...).
i18nContributing.howItWorks3: Keys should be stable. Don’t change keys unless you’re intentionally refactoring text across the app.
i18nContributing.recommendedToolBody: We recommend using Weblate (web UI) connected to GitHub. It allows anyone to translate without touching git, and it opens PRs automatically.
i18nContributing.recommendedTool1: Translate using a browser (no git required).
i18nContributing.recommendedTool2: Maintainers review and merge Weblate PRs.
i18nContributing.githubFallbackBody: If you’re comfortable with git/GitHub, you can also open a PR editing the JSON message files directly.
i18nContributing.rule2: Keep meaning accurate; avoid overly literal translations if they read unnaturally.
i18nContributing.rule3: Don’t translate technical identifiers like file paths, API paths, or code.
